Imagine you are a market maker who has made a deal with Lighter

Lighter lends you 10M LIT, and in return you places $20M into the order book. The agreement is that after one year, you return around 80-90% of the lit

Which scenario is more profitable for you: buying it back at $3 per lit or at $0.5 per lit?

bad tokenomics, shady mm deals, that’s why $LIT keeps trending downward

The volume on Extended has dropped because for months it hasn’t been worth farming (i.e. trading specifically just to earn points)

Because of this, some people in ct are portraying it negatively due to the decline in volume.

But this is actually the right and arguably the only direction. 
Farming are not organic and doesn’t add any real value. Very few have taken this step so far, and in the long run it will have a very positive impact.
